# services.nsd

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.nsd`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.nsd.bind8Stats` | `boolean` | Whether to enable BIND8 like statistics. |
| `services.nsd.dnssecInterval` | `string` | How often to check whether dnssec key rollover is required |
| `services.nsd.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable NSD authoritative DNS server. |
| `services.nsd.extraConfig` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Extra nsd config. |
| `services.nsd.hideVersion` | `boolean` | Whether NSD should answer VERSION.BIND and VERSION.SERVER CHAOS class queries. |
| `services.nsd.identity` | `string` | Identify the server (CH TXT ID.SERVER entry). |
| `services.nsd.interfaces` | `list of string` | What addresses the server should listen to. |
| `services.nsd.ipFreebind` | `boolean` | Whether to bind to nonlocal addresses and interfaces that are down. Similar to ip-transparent. |
| `services.nsd.ipTransparent` | `boolean` | Allow binding to non local addresses. |
| `services.nsd.ipv4` | `boolean` | Whether to listen on IPv4 connections. |
| `services.nsd.ipv4EDNSSize` | `signed integer` | Preferred EDNS buffer size for IPv4. |
| `services.nsd.ipv6` | `boolean` | Whether to listen on IPv6 connections. |
| `services.nsd.ipv6EDNSSize` | `signed integer` | Preferred EDNS buffer size for IPv6. |
| `services.nsd.keys` | `attribute set of (submodule)` | Define your TSIG keys here. |
| `services.nsd.keys.<name>.algorithm` | `string` | Authentication algorithm for this key. |
| `services.nsd.keys.<name>.keyFile` | `absolute path` | Path to the file which contains the actual base64 encoded key. The key will be copied into "/var/lib/nsd/private" before NSD starts. The copied file is only accessibly by the NSD user. |
| `services.nsd.logTimeAscii` | `boolean` | Log time in ascii, if false then in unix epoch seconds. |
| `services.nsd.nsid` | `null or string` | NSID identity (hex string, or "ascii_somestring"). |
| `services.nsd.port` | `16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | Port the service should bind do. |
| `services.nsd.ratelimit.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable ratelimit capabilities. |
| `services.nsd.ratelimit.ipv4PrefixLength` | `null or signed integer` | IPv4 prefix length. Addresses are grouped by netblock. |
| `services.nsd.ratelimit.ipv6PrefixLength` | `null or signed integer` | IPv6 prefix length. Addresses are grouped by netblock. |
| `services.nsd.ratelimit.ratelimit` | `signed integer` | Max qps allowed from any query source. 0 means unlimited. With an verbosity of 2 blocked and unblocked subnets will be logged. |
| `services.nsd.ratelimit.size` | `signed integer` | Size of the hashtable. More buckets use more memory but lower the chance of hash hash collisions. |
| `services.nsd.ratelimit.slip` | `null or signed integer` | Number of packets that get discarded before replying a SLIP response. 0 disables SLIP responses. 1 will make every response a SLIP response. |
| `services.nsd.ratelimit.whitelistRatelimit` | `signed integer` | Max qps allowed from whitelisted sources. 0 means unlimited. Set the rrl-whitelist option for specific queries to apply this limit instead of the default to them. |
| `services.nsd.remoteControl.controlCertFile` | `absolute path` | Path to the client certificate signed with the server certificate. This file is used by nsd-control and generated by nsd-control-setup. |
| `services.nsd.remoteControl.controlKeyFile` | `absolute path` | Path to the client private key, which is used by nsd-control but not by the server. This file is generated by nsd-control-setup. |
| `services.nsd.remoteControl.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable remote control via nsd-control. |
| `services.nsd.remoteControl.interfaces` | `list of string` | Which interfaces NSD should bind to for remote control. |
| `services.nsd.remoteControl.port` | `16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | Port number for remote control operations (uses TLS over TCP). |
| `services.nsd.remoteControl.serverCertFile` | `absolute path` | Path to the server self signed certificate, which is used by the server but and by nsd-control. This file is generated by nsd-control-setup. |
| `services.nsd.remoteControl.serverKeyFile` | `absolute path` | Path to the server private key, which is used by the server but not by nsd-control. This file is generated by nsd-control-setup. |
| `services.nsd.reuseport` | `boolean` | Whether to enable SO_REUSEPORT on all used sockets. This lets multiple processes bind to the same port. This speeds up operation especially if the server count is greater than one and makes fast restarts less prone to fail |
| `services.nsd.rootServer` | `boolean` | Whether this server will be a root server (a DNS root server, you usually don't want that). |
| `services.nsd.roundRobin` | `boolean` | Whether to enable round robin rotation of records. |
| `services.nsd.serverCount` | `signed integer` | Number of NSD servers to fork. Put the number of CPUs to use here. |
| `services.nsd.statistics` | `null or signed integer` | Statistics are produced every number of seconds. Prints to log. If null no statistics are logged. |
| `services.nsd.tcpCount` | `signed integer` | Maximum number of concurrent TCP connections per server. |
| `services.nsd.tcpQueryCount` | `signed integer` | Maximum number of queries served on a single TCP connection. 0 means no maximum. |
| `services.nsd.tcpTimeout` | `signed integer` | TCP timeout in seconds. |
| `services.nsd.verbosity` | `signed integer` | Verbosity level. |
| `services.nsd.version` | `null or string` | The version string replied for CH TXT version.server and version.bind queries. Will use the compiled package version on null. See hideVersion for enabling/disabling this responses. |
| `services.nsd.xfrdReloadTimeout` | `signed integer` | Number of seconds between reloads triggered by xfrd. |
| `services.nsd.zonefilesCheck` | `boolean` | Whether to check mtime of all zone files on start and sighup. |
| `services.nsd.zonefilesWrite` | `signed integer` | Write changed secondary zones to their zonefile every N seconds. If the zone (pattern) configuration has "" zonefile, it is not written. Zones that have received zone transfer updates are written to their zonefile. 0 disables writing to zone files. |
| `services.nsd.zones` | `attribute set of (submodule)` | Define your zones here. Zones can cascade other zones and therefore inherit settings from parent zones. Look at the definition of children to learn about inheritance and child zones. The given example will define 3 zones (example.(com|org|net).). Both example.com. and example.org. inherit their configuration from serverGroup1. |
| `services.nsd.zones.<name>.allowAXFRFallback` | `boolean` | If NSD as secondary server should be allowed to AXFR if the primary server does not allow IXFR. |
| `services.nsd.zones.<name>.allowNotify` | `list of string` | Listed primary servers are allowed to notify this secondary server. Format: `<ip> <key-name \| NOKEY \| BLOCKED>` `<ip>` either a plain IPv4/IPv6 address or range. Valid patters for ranges: * `10.0.0.0/24`: via subnet size * `10.0.0.0&255.255.255.0`: via subnet mask * `10.0.0.1-10.0.0.254`: via range A optional port number could be added with a '@': * `2001:1234::1@1234` `<key-name \| NOKEY \| BLOCKED>` * `<key-name>` will use the specified TSIG key * `NOKEY` no TSIG signature is required * `BLOCKED`notifies from non-listed or blocked IPs will be ignored |
| `services.nsd.zones.<name>.children` | `attribute set of anything` | Children zones inherit all options of their parents. Attributes defined in a child will overwrite the ones of its parent. Only leaf zones will be actually served. This way it's possible to define maybe zones which share most attributes without duplicating everything. This mechanism replaces nsd's patterns in a save and functional way. |
| `services.nsd.zones.<name>.data` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | The actual zone data. This is the content of your zone file. Use imports or pkgs.lib.readFile if you don't want this data in your config file. |
| `services.nsd.zones.<name>.dnssec` | `boolean` | Whether to enable DNSSEC. |
| `services.nsd.zones.<name>.dnssecPolicy.algorithm` | `string` | Which algorithm to use for DNSSEC |
| `services.nsd.zones.<name>.dnssecPolicy.coverage` | `string` | The length of time to ensure that keys will be correct; no action will be taken to create new keys to be activated after this time. |
| `services.nsd.zones.<name>.dnssecPolicy.keyttl` | `string` | TTL for dnssec records |
| `services.nsd.zones.<name>.dnssecPolicy.ksk` | `submodule` | Key policy for key signing keys |
| `services.nsd.zones.<name>.dnssecPolicy.ksk.keySize` | `signed integer` | Key size in bits |
| `services.nsd.zones.<name>.dnssecPolicy.ksk.postPublish` | `string` | How long after deactivation to keep a key in the zone |
| `services.nsd.zones.<name>.dnssecPolicy.ksk.prePublish` | `string` | How long in advance to publish new keys |
| `services.nsd.zones.<name>.dnssecPolicy.ksk.rollPeriod` | `string` | How frequently to change keys |
| `services.nsd.zones.<name>.dnssecPolicy.zsk` | `submodule` | Key policy for zone signing keys |
| `services.nsd.zones.<name>.dnssecPolicy.zsk.keySize` | `signed integer` | Key size in bits |
| `services.nsd.zones.<name>.dnssecPolicy.zsk.postPublish` | `string` | How long after deactivation to keep a key in the zone |
| `services.nsd.zones.<name>.dnssecPolicy.zsk.prePublish` | `string` | How long in advance to publish new keys |
| `services.nsd.zones.<name>.dnssecPolicy.zsk.rollPeriod` | `string` | How frequently to change keys |
| `services.nsd.zones.<name>.maxRefreshSecs` | `null or signed integer` | Limit refresh time for secondary zones. This is the timer which checks to see if the zone has to be refetched when it expires. Normally the value from the SOA record is used, but this option restricts that value. |
| `services.nsd.zones.<name>.maxRetrySecs` | `null or signed integer` | Limit retry time for secondary zones. This is the timeout after a failed fetch attempt for the zone. Normally the value from the SOA record is used, but this option restricts that value. |
| `services.nsd.zones.<name>.minRefreshSecs` | `null or signed integer` | Limit refresh time for secondary zones. |
| `services.nsd.zones.<name>.minRetrySecs` | `null or signed integer` | Limit retry time for secondary zones. |
| `services.nsd.zones.<name>.multiMasterCheck` | `boolean` | If enabled, checks all masters for the last zone version. It uses the higher version from all configured masters. Useful if you have multiple masters that have different version numbers served. |
| `services.nsd.zones.<name>.notify` | `list of string` | This primary server will notify all given secondary servers about zone changes. Format: `<ip> <key-name \| NOKEY>` `<ip>` a plain IPv4/IPv6 address with on optional port number (ip@port) `<key-name \| NOKEY>` - `<key-name>` sign notifies with the specified key - `NOKEY` don't sign notifies |
| `services.nsd.zones.<name>.notifyRetry` | `signed integer` | Specifies the number of retries for failed notifies. Set this along with notify. |
| `services.nsd.zones.<name>.outgoingInterface` | `null or string` | This address will be used for zone-transfer requests if configured as a secondary server or notifications in case of a primary server. Supply either a plain IPv4 or IPv6 address with an optional port number (ip@port). |
| `services.nsd.zones.<name>.provideXFR` | `list of string` | Allow these IPs and TSIG to transfer zones, addr TSIG|NOKEY|BLOCKED address range 192.0.2.0/24, 1.2.3.4&255.255.0.0, 3.0.2.20-3.0.2.40 |
| `services.nsd.zones.<name>.requestXFR` | `list of string` | Format: `[AXFR\|UDP] <ip-address> <key-name \| NOKEY>` |
| `services.nsd.zones.<name>.rrlWhitelist` | `list of (one of "nxdomain", "error", "referral", "any", "rrsig", "wildcard", "nodata", "dnskey", "positive", "all")` | Whitelists the given rrl-types. |
| `services.nsd.zones.<name>.zoneStats` | `null or string` | When set to something distinct to null NSD is able to collect statistics per zone. All statistics of this zone(s) will be added to the group specified by this given name. Use "%s" to use the zones name as the group. The groups are output from nsd-control stats and stats_noreset. |
